markdown:
#  Who can view a 'Draft' RFI? 
##  Answer 
An RFI in the 'Draft' state can only be viewed by a limited number of people.
### If You Create an RFI as a 'Draft'
'Draft' RFIs that are not marked 'Private' are visible to:
  * The RFI's creator
  * The RFI Manager
  * Users with 'Admin' level permissions on the project's RFIs tool


'Draft' RFIs that are marked 'Private' are visible to:
  * The RFI's creator
  * The RFI Manager
  * Users with 'Admin' level permissions on the project's RFIs tool
  * Users with the ['View Private RFIs Associated to Users within Same Company' granular permission](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/permissions-company/tutorials/grant-granular-permissions-in-a-project-permissions-template) enabled on their permissions template (if another user in their company [including them] is the RFI's creator or is designated as the RFI Manager, an Assignee, or a Distribution List member)


#####  Note
  * Users who are designated as the RFI's 'Assignees' or who have been added as a member of its 'Distribution List' will not be able to view the 'Draft' RFI until it is marked 'Open'. See [Edit an RFI](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/tutorials/edit-an-rfi).
  * A 'Draft' RFI will not appear in the 'My Open Items' area of the Portfolio tool for the person who has the current Ball In Court (BIC) responsibility for the RFI. See [View My Open Items in the Portfolio Tool](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/portfolio-company/tutorials/view-my-open-items-in-the-portfolio-tool).


### If You Mark an RFI as 'Private'
If you want to make an 'Open' or 'Draft' RFI private, mark the **Private** checkbox while creating or editing the RFI. When an RFI is 'Private', it can only be viewed by:
  * The RFI's creator
  * The RFI Manager
  * The RFI's Assignee - 'Open' RFIs only
  * The RFI's Distribution List members - 'Open' RFIs only
  * Users with 'Admin' level permissions on the project's RFIs tool
  * Users with the ['View Private RFIs Associated to Users within Same Company' granular permission](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/permissions-company/tutorials/grant-granular-permissions-in-a-project-permissions-template) enabled on their permissions template (if another user in their company [including them] is the RFI's creator or is designated as the RFI Manager, an Assignee, or a Distribution List member)


To mark new RFIs 'Private' by default, see [Configure Settings: RFIs](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/tutorials/configure-advanced-settings-rfis).

title: Who can view a 'Draft' RFI?
